def: s.m. A spring, source, fount; a fountain; — spectacles ( = ćashmak); — a batten
more: n. m. 1. A spring ( sot ); source. 2. Spectacles ( ainak ). Chāṅdī kā chashmah lagāte haiṅ . He wears silver spectacles. (He takes bribes). 3. Battens. ām chashmah yā hauz . A public reservoir.
subs: 'To wear silver spectacles'; (fig.) to take bribes.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian چَشْمَه (čašma).

def: s.f. Winking, a wink; looking askance (at), coldness, misunderstanding (e.g. merī un-se ćashmak hai); — spectacles; — the seed of Cassia absus (used as a remedy in diseases of the eye; syn. ćāksū)
more: n. f. 1. Wink; winking. 2. Misunderstanding; unpleasantness; rupture; tiff. Merī un se chashmak hai .

s. f. 1. Wink, winking. 2. Spectacles. 3. (called also چاکسو chāksū and قشميزج ḳishmīzaj ) The seed of Cassia absus, used as a remedy in diseases of the eyes. چشمک زدن chashmak-zadan, A winking. چشمک زن chashmak-zan or چشمک زنان chashmak-zanān, Winking; winker. چشمک مارنا chashmak mārnā, To wink.
subs: ćashmak-zanāṅ, adj. & s.m. Winking; — a winker
wety: From Classical Persian چشمک (čašmak, literally “a small eye”).

ety: fr. Zend ćaśh = S. चष्, cf. ćāsht
def: s.f. Taste, flavour, relish; a mixture of sweet and sour; a taste by way of a sample; a specimen; a piece of gold or silver melted to prove its purity; proof, trial; — syrup; the viscous state of a syrup
more: n. f. P. chashīdan , S. चष to taste. 1. The viscous state of a syrup ( pāt ). 2. Taste; relish. 3. A mixture of sweet and sour. 4. A piece of gold or silver melted to prove its purity. 5. A pan in which the juice of the sugar-cane is boiled. chāshnī-dār . Sweet and sour.
s. f. 1. Flavour, relish, a mixture of sweet and sour. 2. A specimen. 3. Sirup.
subs: s.m. A taster; a servant whose duty it is to watch over the kitchen of princes and to taste every dish brought on table (as a security against poison; cf. bakāwal).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian چاشنی (čāšnī).

def: s.f. = ćāsnī, q.v. s.f. A large pan in which sugar-cane juice is boiled.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian چاشنی (čāšnī).

def: s.f. The middle hour between sunrise and the meridian; the meal eaten at that hour, breakfast; dinner, a collation
more: s. f. The middle hour between sunrise and the meridian; also, what is eaten at that time, breakfast, a collation.

def: s.m. A ploughman, &c. ( = ćāsā, q.v.); — s.f. ćās(nā)+S. (i)tavyā+tā+ikā), Ploughing, &c. ( = ćās, q.v.)
s.m. Taste (for), relish; longing, ardent desire; prurience, itching; habit, custom
more: n. m. 1. Taste; relish; zest; gusto. 1. Baiṭhe baiṭhe āp se kar baiṭhtā hūṅ kuchh gunāh, Pāoṅ paṛne kā jo uske mujh ko chaskā ho gayā . Jurat. I sit and plot and still find something wrong to do, Such pleasure 'tis to clasp her feet and sue anew! 2. Ab tum suno isak kā chaskā, Laiṅhgā pahne assī gaj kā, Nāṛā ḍāle pandrah laṛ kā . Illit. Now listen all what taste of love will do, A skirt full eighty yards displayed to view, A braid of fifteen cords suspended too! 2. Habit ( bān , 3). chaskā paṛnā yā lagnā , v. n. To acquire a taste for.
s. m. Love, ardent desire, a relish, habit, custom.
subs: ćaskā lagnā, ćaskā ho-jānā (-kā), To acquire or have a taste (for), &c.

def: v.t. To cause to pain or throb.
direct plural; oblique/vocative singular
oblique plural of چَشْمَہ (caśma)

ety: ćās˚, prob. fr. S. चकर्ष˚, by redupl. fr. rt. कृष्
def: v.t. To plough, till, cultivate.
v.n. To burst or split (as tight cloth); to be torn or rent (syn. masaknā; phasaknā).
more: v. n. To be sucked, absorbed.
v. n. To burst, or split (as, tight cloth).

def: s.f. A large pan in which sugar-cane juice is boiled.

def: adj. Sticking (to), adhesive, viscous, slimy; coherent; congruous; applicable, suitable, to the point
more: adj. Viscous, slimy, coherent, sticking together.
subs: v.t. To join or cause to stick (to, -par), to stick, affix (on, — as a paper or notice on a board or wall, &c.).

def: s.f. Sticking together, adhesiveness, viscosity.

def: part. adj. Stuck (to), glued, cemented, adhered; attached (to), inclined (to), addicted (to), fond (of), liking.
more: (from چسپيدن) adj. 1. Joined to, stuck, adhered. 2. Addicted to, liking, fond of, attached.

def: s.f. Adhesion, tenacity, coherence; attachment, love, affection.
more: (from چسپيدن) s. f. Love, affection, attachment, coherence, tenacity.

def: s.f. A cutaneous disease peculiar to the palms of the hands and the soles of the feet (said to be occasioned by insects); a kind of leprosy.
more: s. f. A species of itch peculiar to the palms of the hands and soles of the feet, said to be occasioned by insects.
s. f. The juice of fruits.

def: s.m. A cheat, a rogue.
more: s. f. 1. Longing, wish, itch, relish, taste. 2. Habit, custom. 3. A delicacy, bonne bouche. چاٿ لينا chāṭ lenā, To lick up.
wety: From چَاٹْنَا (cāṭnā, “to lick”); see there for more.

def: s.f. The sound of breaking, or snapping, or cracking; a snap, a crack; — a scratch, a slight cut or wound, an excoriation, a sore, a scab, a chancre
subs: s.f. The sound of repeated snapping or cracking, the sound of the crackling of a burning body, or of quickly repeated strokes, &c.; the sound made in cracking the fingers (cf. ćaṭā-caṭ)
v.n. To break with a snap or crack; to snap short.

ety: contrac. of ćāṭ; see ćāṭnā and ćaṭānā
def: s.f. Eating up, devouring, &c. (used chiefly in comp.); — adv. Wholly, altogether, clean
subs: s.m. Eating up the whole, licking the platter clean
(-ko), To eat with zest; to eat up entirely, to lick the platter clean, to devour, consume; to dissipate; to make away with, to embezzle
v.n. To be eaten up entirely, be swallowed up, be devoured, be consumed; to be dissipated, &c.

ety: v.n. of ćāṭnā, q.v.; and cf. P. ćāsht and ćāshnī
def: s.f. Eating, tasting, licking; lick; taste, flavour; relish; a delicacy, bonne bouche; — wish, longing, prurience; habit, custom
subs: (-kī), To get thetaste (of); to acquire a taste or longing (for)
(-ko), To give one a taste for; to inculcate a habit
wety: From چَاٹْنَا (cāṭnā, “to lick”); see there for more.

def: adv. Quickly, instantly, in a trice
more: s. f. Rag, scrap. چٿ اکهڙنا chiṭ ukhaṛnā, v. n. To splinter or have small pieces knocked off (furniture).

1. (Sans. झटिति ) adv. Quickly, instantly. 2. s. f. An excoriation, a sore, scab, chancre. 3. The noise of breaking, snap. چٿ بهسم chaṭ-bhasam, Eating up the whole, licking the platter clean. چٿ دي ٿوٿنا chaṭ de ṭūṭnā, To snap short, to break. چٿ کرنا chaṭ karnā, To eat, dissipate, make waste. چٿ هونا chaṭ honā, v. n. To be dissipated or consumed.
subs: adv. Quickly, instantly, hastily, in a hurry; abruptly, suddenly, all at once
ćaṭ-ā-paṭī, s.f. Quickness, agility; speed; haste, hurry; suddenness; sudden death; a number of deaths in rapid succession.

def: s.m. The vessel that receives the juice of the sugar-cane as it drops from the mill (cf. ćāṭī).

s.m. Repeated sound of snapping or cracking; the sound of snapping or cracking the finger-joints; the sound of quickly repeated strokes, &c. (see ćaṭ-ćaṭ, s.v. ćaṭ); — adv. Repeatedly, successively, in quick succession.

subs: To spread a mat, to mat.

more: n. m. The pied cuckoo or papīha; cuculus melanoleucos , supposed to drink rain drops only.
s. m. Name of a bird. (Cuculus melanoleucos).

s.f. (m.?), Brightness, brilliance, lustre, splendour; bright colour; glitter; gaudiness; prime of life

s.m. = ćaṭāḵẖ, or ćaṭāk, q.v. s.f. (intens.) Loud cracks or reports; repeated cracks, &c.; also = ćaṭ-ćaṭ, q.v.

ety: Prk. चडक्की and चडक्कओ; S. चट+ क+का and कः

more: (properly چٿاکا) s. m. A crash, an explosion, a smack (or kiss).

def: v.n. = ćaṭaknā, q.v. v.n. To give forth a snapping or cracking sound, to crack, to crackle (as burning wood, &c.); to break, to crack with a report, to split, burst, explode; to burst or open (as a bud), to bloom; to be irritated; to speak snappishly, or angrily, or rudely; a split or dispute to occur, alienation or estrangement to take place (between, -meṅ, two persons, — in this sense, the verb, in the past imperfect tense, is idiomatically used in the feminine, e.g. un donoṅ-meṅ ḵẖūb ćaṭkī), to fall out; to make (oneself) scarce, to make off, to disappear, go away suddenly; (in Urdū the word is commonly written ćaṭaḵẖnā)
more: v. n. To crackle (as, charcoal). See چٿکنا chaṭaknā.

ety: Prk. चडक्क(इ) or चडक्के(इ), fr. S. चट+कृ
def: v.n. To give forth a snapping or cracking sound, to crack, to crackle (as burning wood, &c.); to break, to crack with a report, to split, burst, explode; to burst or open (as a bud), to bloom; to be irritated; to speak snappishly, or angrily, or rudely; a split or dispute to occur, alienation or estrangement to take place (between, -meṅ, two persons, — in this sense, the verb, in the past imperfect tense, is idiomatically used in the feminine, e.g. un donoṅ-meṅ ḵẖūb ćaṭkī), to fall out; to make (oneself) scarce, to make off, to disappear, go away suddenly; (in Urdū the word is commonly written ćaṭaḵẖnā)
more: v. n. 1. To crackle (as wood on the fire), to crack (as, a whip). 2. To split.
s. m. A slap, a box, a blow, cuff.

s.f. Rock, rocky ground; a large block of stone.
more: s. f. Rocky ground, block of stone, rock.

def: v.t. To cause to taste; to cause to lick or lap; to put (a sword, &c.) to the grindstone, to sharpen, to whet (a sword, &c.); to give (one) a smack or taste of (a whip; or a douceur, or bribe, &c.); — s.m. The ceremony of feeding a child for the first time.
more: (causal of چاٿنا) v. a. To make lick or lap up.

def: s.f. See s.v. ćaṭ, 'quickly.' s.m. A cheat, a rogue.
s.f. A variegated colour
s.m. A large net, a seine; — a conspiracy (syn. ćor-manḍalī).
more: adj. S. चतुर four; Pālī chaturo . Clever; accomplished; skilful; dextrous. 1. Yār mero chātar, balam mero modhū, Main sagro apno joban gaṅvāë āī rī, 'Nandī, nāch le, āṅgan buhār āī rī!' Rus. song. My lover is so sharp, my husband such an ass, My rounded charms free given and spent from me may pass, 'Dance, sister-in law! the area's swept for you, sweet lass.' (She has had an assignation with her lover under pretence of sweeping the area). 2. Tam chātur Horī ke khilaiyā, Ham ḍarpok anārī, lāj rakh le, Siyām, hamārī! Song. Holī . You are, O Krishn, so clever at this amorous sport! A timid fool I am, my honor but support! 3. Chhināl lugāī, chātur sipāhī! Prov. A loose woman and a gallant soldier! 4. Mūrakh kī sārī rain, chātur kī ek ghaṛī . Prov. One hour with a smart young blade is better than a whole night with a lout. 5. Sone ko māṭī kare phūaṛ māṭī ḍār, Māṭī kā sonā bane, kare chatar wo…
s. m. A large net, a seine.

def: s.f. A species of herb which springs up with the rabīʻ or spring crops. (It is used as fodder for cattle; and the poorer cultivators use the seeds, mixed with barley, for food.)
adj. (f. -ī), = ćaṭpaṭiyā, q.v. adj. Quick, active, brisk, smart, alert; hasty, precipitate; expert, adroit, clever; — hot, pungent; tasty, savoury ( = ćarparā).
v.n. To make a cracking or crackling noise, to make a snap or crash (as anything hard in breaking).
alternative form of چٹگاؤں (caṭgāõ)
ex: اور چاٹگام میں انکل انور کے ہاں رہوں گا
aur cāṭgām mẽ aṅkal anvar ke hā̃ rahūṅgā
And uncle Anwar's "yes" will remain in Chittagong.
wety: Possibly from South Asian Classical Persian چاتگام.

def: Chittagong (a city in Bangladesh); Chittagong District (a district of Bangladesh); Chittagong Division (a division of Bangladesh)
wety: Borrowed from Bengali চাটগাঁও (caṭgãō).

def: s.f. An earthen vessel or pitcher, a 'chatty'; a churn.
s.m. A school-boy, pupil, scholar ( = ćaṭṭā).
more: 1. s. m. A spy to thieves, the head of a gang of thieves. 2 ( चूडा ) s. f. A lock of hair left on the head when the rest is shaved.
s. m. A pupil, a scholar.

ety: S. चर्पट+इका+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि caus. augment
def: v.t. To slap, box, cuff (see ćāṅṭā).
s.m. A parching sun; deficiency, scarcity, dearth, famine; excessive thirst; eager desire
more: s. m. 1. A kind of grass or grain. 2. Mucus, slime.
s. m. Scarcity. چٿکالگانا chaṭkā lagānā, To thirst, to wish.

more: (caus. of چٿکنا) v. a. 1. To crack (as, a whip), to snap the fingers (in rejoicing). 2. To split. 3. To fire off a musket. 4. To irritate.

def: s.f. The clack of the tongue in urging cattle, &c.; the cracking noise made by the finger-joints (when bent and pressed).

adj. (f. -ī), Bright, brilliant, glittering, splendid, glowing; gaudy, showy, gay

def: s.m. Name of a caste of Rājpūts (so called after their first king, ćatur-vāhu).
more: s. m A cast of Rājpūts.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Having four folds; four-fold; consisting of a set of four.
adj. Seventy-four.

s.m. A quadrangle; a square; a court-yard; an open place in a town where the market is held (and where also the chief of the police-office is stationed); a daily market; the main street or central thoroughfare of a city; — a square place filled (at marriages and other occasious of rejoicing) with sweetmeats (which, after certain ceremonies, are distributed); — an ornamented square of coloured meal, &c., in which a bride and bridegroom are seated a short while during a certain number of nights before the wedding; the number four, an aggregate of four (e.g. tīn ćauk bārhā); — a grinder or back-tooth (being one of a set of four)
subs: ćauk pūrnā, To form a square space of coloured meal in which at marriages the bride and bridegroom are seated on chairs, &c.; — to fill a square space with sweetmeats, &c. on some occasion of rejoicing; — to make chequers or squares
s.m. A seat on which people sit and eat in the ceremony of ćauk
ćauk-ćāṅdnī, s.f. A festival held on the fourth of the light fortnight in Bhādoṅ, when the pupils of pātśālas worship Ganeś
v.n. A market to be held; a row of shops to be opened
s.m. A tax on all goods sold in a ćauk.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀘𑀤𑀼𑀓𑁆𑀓 (cadukka), from Sanskrit चतुष्क (catuṣka). Cognate with Bengali চক (cok), Punjabi چَوک (cauk) / ਚੌਕ (cauk), Nepali चोक (cok), Gujarati ચોક (cok), Odia ଚଉକ (cauka).

def: s.m. An aggregate of four, a four (e.g. do ćaukā aṭṭhā); — a square piece of ground; the space in which a Hindū cooks and eats his victuals (the ground being first plastered with mud and cowdung); a square slab of marble or other stone; — the four front teeth; — the cube of the measure called bāṅs
subs: To make a ćaukā (i.e. to plaster and prepare a space for cooking) and scrub the pots and pans.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀘𑀉𑀓𑁆𑀓 (caükka), from Sanskrit चतुष्क (catuṣka).

adj. 1. Cautious, watchful, accurate, diligent, active, clever, alert, circumspect, intelligent. 2. Full weight.

subs: (-ko), To keep watch (over), to watch, to guard; to be vigilant or circumspect (in respect of); to be on the alert.

To watch (for, -kī); to watch, guard, mount guard; to give a seat (to, -ko)

more: n. f. 1. See chaukṛā . 2. A carriage and four. 3. A bound; a spring; a bounce ( chhalāṅg ). chaukṛī bharnā , v. a. To bound; spring; leap; bounce. chaukṛī bhūlnā , v. n. 1. To lose one's elastic bound. 2. To be perplexed, confounded; to come to a dead stop; to be at a loss.

ety: ćauk, q.v.+ṛī = aṛī = Prk. अडिआ = S. र+इका
def: s.f. An aggregate of four; an assembly of four (persons); a carriage and four; a period comprising the four yugas or ages; — bound (as of a deer, &c. — the four legs being all drawn up at once), spring, bounce
more: s. f. 1. A bound, a spring, a bounce. 2. ( S̱. see چوکڙا) A ring of pearls worn by men in the ears. چوکڙي بهرنا chaukaṛī bharnā, To leap, bound, bounce. چوکڙي بهولنا chaukaṛī bhūlnā, To forget oneʼs bound, i. e. to be fascinated, to have oneʼs senses benumbed. چوکڙي مار بيٿهنا chaukaṛī mār baiṭhnā, To squat.
s. (dakh.) An ornament worn by women tight round the neck, formed of stars of gold, strung on three black silk or wire threads, with black beads filling up the interstices ( Qanoon-e-islam ).
subs: v.n. To bound along; to bound, spring, leap, bounce
v.n. To forget (one's) bound, to lose (one's) elastic tread; to be fascinated, be bewildered or confounded, to have (one's) senses benumbed

more: n. f. A weed ( amaranthus polygamus ); wild rice; a red potherb ( lāl chaulāī, marsā ); a green potherb ( harī chaulāī, gander ).

adj. (prov.) = ćanćal, q.v. adj. & s.m. more: adj. 1. Brisk; agile; active. Baṛī chauṅchāl aurat hai . 2. Clever; sharp.

r: će, ćīm, Urdū, ća, Hindī, Hindūstānī, Nāgarī, ch, cherry, jīm-ě-fārsī, jīm-ě-ʻajamī, jīm, abjad, ća-kār
def: s.m. The letter or the sound ća.
more: Persian, called also jīm-i-fārsī or jīm-i-ajmī , the seventh letter of the Persian alphabet, sounds like ch in the English word cheek. It does not occur in Arabic. In abjad q. v. it stands for the number (3), the same as jīm . च cha H. is the sixth consonant in the Hindi alphabet, and the first letter of the chavarg or palatal class. As initial, medial, and final it is interchangable with k , as chasak=kasak ache; chillānā=killānā to squeal; chāchā=kākā uncle; chūṅ=kyoṅ why; chhāl=khāl skin; lachchhan=lakkhan manner; achchhar=akkhar letter; māchhī=mākhī a fly: with sh , as chhaṭ = S. shashṭ , P. shash six; H. chhāyā = Eng. shadow; bichhaṛnā = Brij. bishaṭnā to be separated; P. la/?/khchah = P. la/?/khshah live coal: with p, t, ṭ and j , q. v. As an initial with ḍ , as chapnī=ḍhaknī a cover: with s , as chhobā=sobhā splendour; chhakṛā = Panj. saëḍho cart; chatrāo = satrāo shower: chhajnā …

called also jīm-i-fārsī or jīm-i-aﻌjamī, the seventh letter of the Persian alphabet, sounds like ch in the English word cheek. It does not occur in Arabic. If used to denote a number, according to the abjad, it stands for 3, like ج ( jīm ). The permutations of the letter چ are the following; into ژ as کاژ for کاچ Glass, a pine tree; into ز as پزشک pizishk for پچشک pichishk, A physician or apothecary; into ش as کاشي for کاچي Made of glass; into ج as کاج for کاچ; and, arabistically, into ص as صين for چين China. This letter corresponds to च in Sanskrit, and when aspirated, or with ه affixed, to छ .
